Off Calendar Dec-09-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Philip S. Ward, Esq., of HASSARD BONNINGTON (415-288-9800), appearing for defendant / moving party General Cable Corporation
Anna M. Costa, Esq. of LEVIN SIMES KAISER & GORNICK LLP (415-646-7160), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Lynn R. Baranco, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT GENERAL CABLE CORPORATION's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Parties submitted to the Court's ruling and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant General Cable Corporation's motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication on plaintiffs' strict liability claims is granted. Defendant did not satisfy its burden of production that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Ms. Baranco was exposed to an asbestos-containing product or material attributable to defendant. The deposition testimony of Mr. Baranco and Mr. Katz preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Strict liability does not apply to plaintiffs' claims against defendant since the undisputed facts establish that Ms. Baranco was not exposed to an asbestos-containing product or material attributable to defendant that was placed in the stream of commerce or that had been sold to a consumer. Prevailing party is to prepare an order.Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (220/HEK)

RULING:
Plaintiffs LYNN BARANCO, LISA SMITH, LESLIE MARTIN and LYNN BIBB's motion to compel the deposition of defendant General Cable Corporation's person most knowledgeable and custodian of records, to compel the production of documents, and for monetary and issue sanctions. Argued and tentative ruling adopted as modified: Plaintiffs' motion is granted in part and denied in part. As to each category, General Cable is required to identify the person(s) most knowledgeable as the categories of information sought in plaintiff's deposition notice within 45 days from FEB-18-2010. If Mr. Gable is the sole person most knowledgeable as to any category and General Cable represents in writing that Mr. Gable has no further information that what he stated in his deposition, there will be no need for any further deposition as to that category. However, as to all other categories, a further deposition is warranted. General Cable must also make a diligent effort to locate all requested documents and provide plaintiffs with a verified response stating what documents it has been able to locate and must do so within 30 days. Within 45 days from FEB-18-2010 General Cable will produce all repsonsive documents to plaintiffs. Plaintiff's request for monetary sanctions is granted in the amount of $500 to be paid to plaintiff within 30 days. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Matter not reported
